deep_dive.title: Teaching LLMs String Matching, Backtracking, and Error Recovery to Deduce Bases and Truth Tables for the Combinatorially Exploding Bit Manipulation Puzzles
deep_dive.abstract: This paper presents our algorithmic innovations for the NVIDIA Nemotron Model Reasoning Challenge, focusing on Bit Manipulation Puzzles. In this task, the objective is to discover a hidden logical rule transforming input binary strings to outputs, then apply it to unseen inputs. Large Language Models (LLMs) notoriously struggle here; traditional methods force them to simulate complex boolean logic and arithmetic, leading to hallucinations. Furthermore, the search space of bitwise operations (combinations of shifts, rotations, and logic gates) suffers from a severe combinatorial explosion. To overcome this computational intractability, we present a novel approach that abandons arithmetic logic entirely in favor of string similarity, structured search, and autonomous error recovery.   Our core contributions are: 1. Bases and Truth Table Formulation: We reframe logic-gate deduction into a base-selection task, leveraging string similarity (minimal bit flips) to isolate primitive transformations ("bases") and deduce truth tables without complex arithmetic. 2. Backtracking DFS and Error Recovery: We formalize a search process that tests candidate bases, detects logical collisions across examples, and backtracks upon failure to perform robust error recovery. 3. Bit Tokenization and Interactive Reasoning SFT: We force the tokenizer to encode binary strings as individual single-bit tokens. We use dynamic masking to simulate external oracle feedback, training the model to hypothesize, self-evaluate, and backtrack natively.   Evaluated on bit manipulation puzzles, our approach achieved over 96% validation accuracy. This represents the highest performance in this category, driving our 7th Place overall finish in the contest.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: teaches LLMs to deduce truth tables and boolean logic for bit manipulation puzzles, relevant to hardware formal verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 教授大语言模型推导位操作谜题的真值表和布尔逻辑，与硬件形式验证相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 教授大语言模型推导位操作谜题的真值表和布尔逻辑，与硬件形式验证相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 教授大语言模型推导位操作谜题的真值表和布尔逻辑，与硬件形式验证相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents algorithmic innovations to teach LLMs string matching, backtracking, and error recovery for deducing hidden logical rules and truth tables from bit manipulation puzzles, overcoming combinatorial explosion.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对大语言模型在布尔逻辑推理中的困难，提出了结合字符串匹配、回溯和错误恢复的创新算法，使LLM能够从二进制输入输出中推导隐藏的逻辑规则和真值表，有效解决了位操作谜题中的组合爆炸问题。该成果可直接应用于硬件形式验证中的逻辑函数推断和等价性检查。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对大语言模型在布尔逻辑推理中的困难，提出了结合字符串匹配、回溯和错误恢复的创新算法，使LLM能够从二进制输入输出中推导隐藏的逻辑规则和真值表，有效解决了位操作谜题中的组合爆炸问题。该成果可直接应用于硬件形式验证中的逻辑函数推断和等价性检查。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: large language model for hardware formal verification

quick_skim.title: Automated Semantic Fault Localization in SysML v2: A Human-in-the-Loop Framework Using Knowledge-Graph Augmented LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: SysML v2's textual syntax enables compiler-based validation of model structure and language conformance. However, semantic mistakes that preserve syntactic validity but violate domain rules cannot be detected through compilers. These errors can propagate through the design process and surface late as costly integration failures. This paper presents a human-in-the-loop framework for identifying and repairing such errors automatically. It combines a fine-tuned Small Language Model (SLM) with a domain knowledge graph encoding physical compatibility rules between system elements. The knowledge graph also guides the generation of synthetic training data by systematically introducing plausible domain violations, and augments the model at inference time to ground repair suggestions in valid engineering constraints. We demonstrate the framework using the vehicle systems domain, where the knowledge graph captures the relationships between the mechanical, electrical, fluid, and signal interfaces. Two SLMs, Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B and DeepSeek-Coder-6.7B, are fine-tuned to output unified diff patches that localize faults and present candidate repairs for engineer review, preserving human judgment in the design process. Evaluation of 1,184 test samples shows that fine-tuning improves semantic fault repair from less than 3% to more than 91%, with patch-based output reducing token length by over 60%. The framework offers a practical path toward AI-assisted model verification that complements existing MBSE tools.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses LLMs with knowledge graph for automated fault localization in system models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大语言模型和知识图谱实现系统模型中的自动故障定位
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用大语言模型和知识图谱实现系统模型中的自动故障定位
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用大语言模型和知识图谱实现系统模型中的自动故障定位
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a human-in-the-loop framework combining fine-tuned Small Language Model with domain knowledge graph to detect and repair semantic faults in SysML v2 system models, applicable to hardware design verification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对SysML v2系统模型中语法正确但违反领域规则的语义错误，提出了一个人机协同的自动定位与修复框架。该框架结合了微调的小语言模型和编码物理兼容性规则的知识图谱，并利用知识图谱生成合成训练数据。实验表明该方法能有效检测并修复语义故障，可用于系统级硬件设计验证中。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对SysML v2系统模型中语法正确但违反领域规则的语义错误，提出了一个人机协同的自动定位与修复框架。该框架结合了微调的小语言模型和编码物理兼容性规则的知识图谱，并利用知识图谱生成合成训练数据。实验表明该方法能有效检测并修复语义故障，可用于系统级硬件设计验证中。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索关于大语言模型在硬件形式验证中应用的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: large language model for hardware formal verification; hardware formal verification using large language models; RTL verification with LLMs; What are the latest advances in applying LLMs to formal verification of RTL designs?; Can LLMs assist in model checking and theorem proving for hardware design?.
